figures.title: Is Trend Still Your Friend?: A Microstructural Account of the Demise of Short-Term Trend-Following
figures.caption: Figure 1: (Rebased) SG CTA Index, 2000–2025. Data courtesy of Soci´et´e G´en´erale Prime Services & Clearing. — This line chart displays the Société Générale CTA (Commodity Trading Advisor) Index rebased from 2000 to 2025, showing the cumulative performance of trend-following strategies over a 25-year period. The y-axis measures the index value in basis points (ranging from approximately 100 to 300), while the x-axis shows time from 2000 through 2024. The figure highlights two major disruptions

figures.title: Is Trend Still Your Friend?: A Microstructural Account of the Demise of Short-Term Trend-Following
figures.caption: Fig. 2 (left) shows the cumulative PnL of a fast trend portfolio (EWM-5-20) from 1950 to 2025. Two features stand out: the cumulative PnL is essentially flat from 2009 onward, although a weakening may already be detected from approximately 2000. The five-year rolling Sharpe ratio (right) collapses from a historical range of 1–2.5 to a level statistically indistinguishable from zero post-2010. figures.title: Is Trend Still Your Friend?: A Microstructural Account of the Demise of Short-Term Trend-Following
figures.caption: Fig. 4 disaggregates the cumulative PnL by sector. Trend has effectively vanished for IDX and FXR, while YLD and CMD show no appreciable degradation. figures.title: Liquidity Premium and Investment Horizons
figures.caption: Figure 1 summarizes the end-to-end construction process: starting from raw CRSP daily and monthly files, we apply the cleaning and filtering steps in Section 4.2, construct daily order-flow and price-impact variables (Section 4.3), aggregate to the firm-month level, merge in delisting returns and point-in-time index membership, and assemble the final panel used throughout Sections 6–9. — This table presents summary statistics for monthly stock returns across multiple percentiles and distribution

figures.title: Liquidity Premium and Investment Horizons
figures.caption: Figure 1: Data pipeline from raw CRSP files to firm–month features and final analysis panel. — Table 4 presents summary statistics for illiquidity measures derived from Kyle's lambda regression and Amihud (2002b) liquidity metric, showing the distribution across percentiles with median near zero (0.0009), high kurtosis (4337.5), and substantial right skew (max of 74.1961). figures.title: Settlement Manipulation in Prediction Markets
figures.caption: Figure 2. The five-stage timeline.
